title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold work together to reduce inflammation, soothe irritated skin, and calm the itching sensation. Additionally, pot marigold's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections that can exacerbate itchiness. As a result, using pot marigold tinctures topically or taking it orally can provide rapid relief from itchy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for eczema, psoriasis, and other skin conditions characterized by itching and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by irritated or inflamed skin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture can neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
Antipruritic: Pot marigold tincture's antipruritic properties can directly reduce itching by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers itchiness in the body.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce itching and inflammation associated with skin conditions.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): As a potent antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin helps alleviate itching and discomfort caused by allergic reactions and skin irritation.

Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to reduce itching and inflammation by regulating the immune response and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis for tinctures due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that soothe itchy skin.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can help alleviate itchy skin conditions.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe and calm itchy skin, making them a viable option for tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to preserve their medicinal properties.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which calm irritated skin and reduce itching sensations. The aloe vera gel contains compounds like aloin and barbaloin that have been shown to inhibit histamine release, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating itching. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting healthy skin barrier function and relieving discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation and itching by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
Antioxidants: Aloe vera's antioxidants, including vitamin C, E, and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage and irritation, reducing itching and discomfort.

Soothing mucilage: Aloe vera's soothing mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance, forms a barrier on the skin surface, calming itchiness by moisturizing and protecting the skin from further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a anthraquinone compound, has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation of the skin.
Aloin: Aloin, a glycoside compound, acts as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin while preventing infections.

Vitamins and minerals (especially vitamin E and zinc): Aloe vera tinctures are rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin E and zinc, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce skin irritation and itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for tinctures due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and soothing properties.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera are rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to calm and soothe itchy skin, making them a popular choice for tincture production.
Roots: The roots of Aloe vera are valued for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh aloe vera leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and measure out 500 grams.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with 1500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herbal extract contains compounds like histamine and serotonin, which work to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy sensations. Additionally, nettle's natural antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors. By taking a standardized tincture, individuals can experience relief from bothersome itchiness and enjoy smoother, more comfortable skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin irritations, such as eczema or acne.
Antihistamine: Nettle's antihistamine properties block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers itchy reactions in the body, providing relief from itching and allergic reactions.
Sotholistic: Stinging nettle's soothing and moisturizing effects help calm and comfort dry, irritated skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with conditions like atopic dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Stachydrine: A quaternary ammonium alkaloid that helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy skin conditions by inhibiting histamine release and modulating the immune response.

Alantolactone: A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to alleviate itchy skin by reducing inflammation and suppressing the release of histamine, a key mediator of itching.

Aloe-emodin: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (itch-relieving) properties, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Urtica dioica leaves are used because they contain a high concentration of histamine-releasing compounds that help relieve itching and inflammation.
Stems: Urtica dioica stems are used because they contain similar compounds to the leaves, which help to reduce itching and inflammation associated with skin conditions.
Roots: Urtica dioica roots are used because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems when the plant is in full growth to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested urtica dioica parts into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steep one part of urtica dioica in two parts of a 80-proof spirit for four to six weeks in a sealed container.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by itchiness. Additionally, purple coneflower's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial overgrowth, which can exacerbate itching conditions like eczema and dermatitis. By using an herbal tincture made from purple coneflower, individuals may experience a significant reduction in itchy skin symptoms, leading to improved comfort and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compound, cichoric acid, helps reduce inflammation and itching caused by skin irritations or allergic reactions.
Antimicrobial property: The tincture's antimicrobial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause skin infections and itchiness.

Soothing and antioxidant properties: The presence of antioxidants like quercetin and kaempferol in purple coneflower tinctures helps to calm and soothe itchy skin, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds, particularly echinatin and echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce itching and inflammation in the skin.

Phenolic acids: Specifically, caftaric acid and caffeic acid in Echinacea purpurea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe itchy skin and reduce the severity of skin conditions.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides present in Echinacea purpurea, such as arabinogalactan, may stimulate the immune system and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate itchy skin caused by allergic reactions or skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea roots are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to soothe itchy skin.
Flowers: Echinacea flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids, including quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help relieve itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of echinacea purpurea roots and flowers when they are in full bloom in late summer.
Dry the roots and flowers in a warm place for 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Use a ratio of 1 part dried herb to 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar to prepare the tincture.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for proper extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and bottle it in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance, coats the skin, providing a protective barrier that calms irritated skin and reduces itching and inflammation. Additionally, marshmallow root contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy skin cells. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide fast and effective relief for itchy skin, making it an excellent natural remedy for various skin conditions.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow's mucilage coats and soothes itchy skin, reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier against further irritation.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and itching associated with skin irritations.

Antioxidant activity: Marshmallow's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin inflammation and itchiness, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of irritation.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions.
Althaea officinalis flavonoids: These flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate itching and discomfort caused by skin irritations.
Mucilages: These thick, gel-like compounds help form a protective barrier on the skin, reducing dryness and itching, while also exhibiting anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain mucilages, which help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce itching and inflammation.
Barks: They contain compounds that have anti-itch and anti-inflammatory effects, which can provide relief from itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried althaea officinalis root and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass container.
Combine the herb and vodka in the container and seal it tightly with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container.
Label and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains allantoin, a powerful soothing agent that calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, comfrey's antifungal and antibacterial compounds help to combat infections that can contribute to itchiness. When applied topically as a tincture, comfrey's natural ingredients work together to provide fast and effective relief from itchy skin, leaving it feeling calm, cool, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin, a key compound in comfrey tincture, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations.
Saponins: Comfrey tincture contains saponins, which have emollient and demulcent properties that can help to lock in moisture, soothe dry, itchy skin, and protect the skin from further irritation.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in comfrey tincture, particularly symphytine, has antipruritic properties that can directly reduce itching and scratching associated with conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin helps with itchy skin by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with itchy skin conditions.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in Comfrey help with itchy skin by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm irritated skin.

Saponins (specifically, symphytumoside): Saponins in Comfrey help with itchy skin by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help reduce the itching and discomfort associated with skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source used for tinctures due to their high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: They are also commonly used as they contain a higher amount of glycosides, which help in alleviating itchy skin conditions.
Buds: Buds of Symphytum officinale are sometimes used for tinctures, as they are believed to have a more potent effect on itchy skin conditions compared to other parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh roots and leaves of symphytum officinale in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The natural compounds present in the leaves, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and alleviating itching. Additionally, the astringent properties of broad-leaved lime tinctures can help to tighten and shrink swollen skin tissue, providing quick relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate itchiness and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Broad-leaved lime tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, can help soothe and calm itchy skin by reducing swelling and redness.
Antipruritic properties: Broad-leaved lime tincture contains antipruritic compounds that can directly relieve itching sensations, providing fast and effective relief from itchy skin symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, specifically quercetin, help alleviate itchy skin by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
Terpenoids (including linalool and linalyl acetate): The terpenoids present in Tilia platyphyllos have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm and relieve itchy skin.

Phenolic acids (including rosmarinic acid and gallic acid): Phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help mitigate the underlying causes of itchy skin, such as oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Tilia platyphyllos leaves are often used to make tinctures for itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Tilia platyphyllos buds are used for their soothing effects on itchy skin, providing relief from irritation.
Barks: Tilia platyphyllos barks are utilized in tinctures for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers from a reputable supplier.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking jar daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The active compounds in the herb, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching sensations. This can provide relief from conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and bug bites. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help to reduce allergic reactions that contribute to itchy skin. By addressing both underlying inflammation and immune response, St John's Wort tinctures offer a natural and effective approach to soothing itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

Anti-inflammatory action: The bioactive compounds in St John's Wort, including hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations.

Antimicrobial activity: St John's Wort has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat fungal, bacterial, and viral infections that may contribute to itchy skin conditions such as athlete's foot or ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, helping to reduce itching and inflammation associated with skin conditions.

N-feruloylserotonin (also known as Hyperforin's isomer): This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (itch-reducing) effects, which contribute to the soothing properties of Hypericum perforatum for itchy skin.

Flavonoids (including quercetin): Flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antihistaminic properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and itching associated with skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective in relieving itchy skin.
Flowers: The flowers are abundant in naphthoquinones, particularly hyperforin and hypericin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itchy skin.

Stems: The stems of Hypericum perforatum contain bioflavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to their effectiveness in treating itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped flowers with 40-50 percent vodka by volume in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. The glycyrrhizin in licorice root also exhibits antihistaminic effects, blocking histamine release and alleviating itching sensations. Additionally, licorice has been shown to stimulate the production of cortisol, a hormone that helps regulate skin health and reduce inflammation. By addressing these underlying causes of itchiness, herbal licorice tinctures can provide relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which helps to reduce itching and redness associated with skin irritations.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice tinctures contain flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and itchiness.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside present in licorice root, have moisturizing and emollient properties that help to soothe dry, itchy skin, making it an effective natural treatment for skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce inflammation and itching in the skin by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Liquiritigenin: A flavanone that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, helping to soothe and calm itchy skin.
18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid: A triterpenoid acid that has potent anti-inflammatory and antipruritic effects, helping to reduce itching and inflammation in the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which is responsible for the anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate itchy skin.
Barks: The barks are another commonly used part, containing a similar composition to the roots, which helps in reducing inflammation and itching.

Leaves: Although less commonly used compared to roots and barks, the leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are still utilized in some tinctures, offering a milder form of anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra, typically 1-2 kg, in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces, about 1-2 cm in size, to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol as a solvent, using a 1:5 ratio by weight, in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container occasionally to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and flavonoids, have been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and scratching associated with conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, dandelion tincture's antimicrobial activity may help combat underlying infections that can exacerbate itchy skin issues. By addressing the root causes of itchiness, dandelion tincture provides natural relief from uncomfortable and distracting skin symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and allergies.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidants can neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and skin damage, leading to itchiness and discomfort.
Laxative properties: Dandelion tincture's laxative effects can help relieve constipation, which is often linked to itchy skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by skin irritations.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can soothe itchy skin and reduce the severity of allergic reactions.

Kaempferol: Another flavonoid present in Taraxacum officinale, kaempferol has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can help alleviate itching and redness associated with skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help soothe itchy skin.
Roots: They contain taraxasterol, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipruritic effects, reducing itchiness.
Flowers: They are used for their antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate itchy skin and other allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried taraxacum officinale with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hazel tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in hazel leaves have a soothing effect on the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by itching. The astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, which reduces inflammation and calms down itchy skin. Additionally, the antioxidants in hazel tincture work to repair damaged skin cells, promoting healthy skin regeneration and reducing itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Hazelnut tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like oleic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and itching in the skin.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in hazelnut tincture, such as vitamin E and polyphenols, help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate itchy skin conditions.
Soothing properties: Hazelnut tincture has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with eczema, dermatitis, and other skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hazel plant are used to make tinctures as they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy skin.
Buds: Hazel buds are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for reducing itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hazelnut shells and leaves from the corylus avellana plant for 50 grams.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crumbly.
Grind dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 1 part powdered corylus avellana with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate solids from liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. The mucilages in slippery elm work to calm and protect the skin, providing a barrier against moisture loss and irritation. This natural remedy also has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may contribute to itching. As a result, slippery elm tinctures can provide quick relief from itchy skin symptoms, making it a popular natural solution for soothing dry, itchy, and irritated skin.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm tincture contains mucilages, which are gel-like substances that form a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming itchy skin irritations by reducing inflammation and friction.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: Slippery elm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, reducing itchiness and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant contain mucilage, a natural substance that soothes and calms irritated skin, providing relief from itching and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the slippery elm plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ulmus rubra leaves and stems according to local regulations and guidelines.
Clean and chop the harvested material into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped ulmus rubra with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, for optimal tincture strength.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters into a clean amber bottle, discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tannins present in witch hazel have been shown to reduce itching and irritation by calming the skin's natural response to allergic reactions or environmental factors. Additionally, witch hazel's astringent nature helps to dry out weeping skin irritations, providing instant relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and insect bites.
remedy_properties: Astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins and flavonoids present in Witch Hazel help to reduce inflammation, itching, and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing the production of histamine.

Antimicrobial and antifungal properties: The essential oils and other bioactive compounds in Witch Hazel have antimicrobial and antifungal activities that help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchy skin, such as acne, eczema, and ringworm.

Antioxidant and soothing effects: The antioxidants present in Witch Hazel help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin irritation and itching, while its soothing properties calm the skin and reduce discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin irritations.
Buds: Witch Hazel buds are rich in a compound called hamamelitannin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hamamelis virginiana leaves and stems fresh from the wild or cultivate in your garden.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 60-70% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label and date the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive compounds in elder, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, elder's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can exacerbate itchiness. As a result, elder tinctures provide effective relief from itchy skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis, promoting healthy skin and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema, acne, and dermatitis.
Antioxidant properties: Elder's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate itchy skin conditions.
Soothing and cooling effects: Elder tincture has a calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce redness, irritation, and itching associated with skin irritations like sunburns and rashes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to reduce itching and irritation caused by allergic reactions or eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers in late summer or early fall for optimal potency and freshness.
Chop the freshly harvested flowers into small pieces to release their essential oils and flavor compounds.
Combine one part chopped sambucus nigra flowers with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the active compounds from the herbs.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to create a clear sambucus nigra tincture with a 20-30% alcohol content.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains anthocyanins, powerful flavonoids that have been shown to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation. Additionally, blueberries' natural moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and calm dry, itchy skin. By applying the tincture topically, individuals can experience relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis, promoting a healthier and more comfortable complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants like anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to itchy skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic compounds present in blueberry tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Antihistamine and antipruritic properties: Blueberries contain compounds that exhibit antihistaminic and antipruritic activities, which can help to reduce histamine release, alleviate itching, and provide relief from skin irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing itching and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce itching and irritation associated with skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh or dried vaccinium myrtillus berries, preferably in the summer months.
Clean and chop the harvested berries into small pieces to release their essential oils and flavors.
Combine the chopped berries with 200-250 milliliters of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the clear liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut tinctures helps with itchy skin because they contain a compound called aescin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Aescin reduces swelling and itching by preventing histamine release, a chemical that causes allergic reactions. Additionally, horse chestnut tincture's antiseptic properties help to soothe irritated skin and reduce redness. This natural remedy can provide relief from itchy skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and insect bites, making it a popular choice for those seeking an alternative to harsh chemical treatments.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin is a key component of horse chestnut tincture that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps to reduce itchiness and inflammation associated with skin conditions.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in horse chestnut tincture have antioxidant and antihistamine properties, which can help to calm itchy skin and reduce the allergic response that leads to itching.

Tannins: Tannins are astringent compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching by constricting blood vessels and reducing moisture levels in the skin, making them beneficial for skin conditions such as eczema.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the horse chestnut plant are used to create a soothing and anti-inflammatory tincture that helps to calm and relieve itching caused by skin irritations.
Roots: The roots of the horse chestnut plant contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and itchiness in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ash leaves and stems from the plant, ensuring they are free from pests and diseases.
Chop the harvested ash material into small pieces to facilitate extraction of its bioactive compounds.
Combine one part of chopped ash material with four parts of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container, stirring well to ensure even saturation.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks, shaking the container daily and monitoring the tincture's color and consistency.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the final product with its preparation date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins present in English oak extract have a natural astringent effect that reduces redness and swelling, while also helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness. This combination of properties makes English oak tinctures an effective natural remedy for relieving itchy skin caused by conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and allergic reactions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English Oak contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin irritations and allergic reactions.
Antihistamine properties: The tannins present in English Oak have antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate itchiness caused by histamine release, a common response to allergens or irritants.

Softering and calming effects: English Oak's soothing and calming properties can help reduce skin irritation, inflammation, and itching, making it an effective remedy for skin conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and minor burns.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and terpenoids, oak roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing itchy skin conditions.
Barks: The outer bark of the English oak contains phenolic compounds that can help reduce inflammation and itching, while also providing antimicrobial protection to the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of quercus robur in the morning when dew is still present.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 100g of the fresh quercus robur leaves with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as capsaicin, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing instant relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, monk's pepper tinctures have natural astringent properties that help to dry out weeping eczema lesions and reduce the appearance of redness and swelling. This natural remedy effectively calms itchy skin without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine effects: Monk's pepper tincture has been shown to reduce histamine release, which helps alleviate itching and inflammation caused by allergic reactions or skin irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and alkaloids present in monk's pepper have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with itchy skin conditions such as eczema or psoriasis.

Soothing and calming action: Monk's pepper has a gentle, calming effect on the skin, which helps to reduce itching, irritation, and discomfort caused by dry skin, bug bites, or minor cuts and scrapes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for reducing itchiness.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, they help alleviate itching and inflammation associated with skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried cnidium monnieri leaves and stems in a clean glass jar.
Combine one part cnidium monnieri herb with two parts vodka or grain alcohol in the jar.
Store the mixture in a dark, cool place for 4-6 weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the final product in a dark, cool place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with itchy skin because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin present in chamomile calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness. Additionally, chamomile's antihistamine-like effects help to reduce histamine release, which can exacerbate itching. As a natural relaxant, chamomile also reduces stress-induced inflammation, allowing the skin to heal and calm down. This gentle and non-irritating herbal remedy provides relief from itchy skin conditions, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by irritated skin conditions like eczema or psoriasis.
Antihistamine-like effects: The flavonoids in chamomile have a calming effect on the skin, which can relieve itchy skin sensations caused by allergic reactions or insect bites.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with itchy skin, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can exacerbate skin conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps reduce itching and inflammation by binding to histamine receptors, providing relief from skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn in mid-summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air drying method taking 7-10 days.
Chop the dried chamomile flowers into small pieces and mix with vodka or ethanol 80% to 20%.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks shaking daily in a dark glass container with lid at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy skin are liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and spices that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These tinctures help to calm and relieve itchy skin by reducing inflammation, itching, and scratching, providing instant relief and comfort. For example, oatmeal tincture has been shown to soothe itchy skin caused by eczema, while tea tree oil tincture can help with fungal infections like ringworm. By using these herbal tinctures, people suffering from itchy skin can finally find relief and enjoy a better quality of life, free from the constant discomfort and stress that comes with itchy skin.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy skin that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal remedies such as calendula, chamomile, and aloe vera can soothe and calm irritated skin. Tinctures of burdock root, dandelion, and yellow dock can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with these conditions. Additionally, tinctures of lavender and peppermint can provide relief from mild to moderate symptoms of skin irritation and itching.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with itchy skin is a blend of Calendula, Aloe Vera, and Tea Tree. Calendula tincture soothes and calms irritated skin, while Aloe Vera helps to hydrate and moisturize, reducing inflammation. Tea Tree tincture, with its antifungal and antibacterial properties, fights off any underlying infections that may be causing the itch. Together, these three tinctures create a powerful and natural remedy for itchy skin, promoting healing and comfort.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Chinese medicine, herbal tinctures can be effective in treating itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties. The study analyzed a compound traditional Chinese medicine dermatitis ointment (CTCMDO) made from a mixture of extracts from five plants, which contained 44 compounds including phenylpropionic acid compounds, alkaloid compounds, curcumin compounds, and lignans. The results showed that CTCMDO suppressed DNCB-induced scratching behavior in mice in a dose-dependent manner and effectively improved atopic dermatitis by inhibiting the production of Th1/2 cytokines and itch-related mediators.
